# Research - 2026-06-06

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins, agents, permissions)

> Continuation of 2026-06-02. The 06-02 pass identified five new gaps:
> shell-startup + build-tool config protection (Item #1), `ALEXI_PLUGIN_RULE_TIMEOUT_MS`
> env override (#2), typed `TimeoutError` for shell/network timeouts (#3),
> `examples/plugins/env-blocker/` reference plugin (#4), and `/plugins`+`/skills`
> REPL slash commands (#5). **Items #2 and #5 are now in `src/`.** Verified:
> `runRuleCommand` reads `ALEXI_PLUGIN_RULE_TIMEOUT_MS` with the correct
> precedence (per-call > env > 5000) - tests at
> `src/plugin/__tests__/ruleCommandRunner.test.ts:269-336`; `/plugins` and
> `/skills` REPL commands with `toggle <name>` subcommand and help-text entries
> are live in `src/cli/interactive.ts:240-245,316,2115-2196`. **Items #1, #3,
> #4 remain open.** This pass integrates claude-code v2.1.161-v2.1.167 (the
> CHANGELOG appended six full release sections after 2.1.160), cline v3.87.0
> -> v3.88.0 + CLI v3.0.16-v3.0.20 (#11161, #11211, #11220, #11225, #11227,
> #11230, #11234, #11235, #11240, #11244, #11247, #11256, #11258, #11259,
> #11265, #11273, #11280, #11285, #11291, #11315, #11316, #11318), and
> kilocode v7.3.33 -> v7.3.40 (most are TUI / VS-Code-extension specific).
> aider (no in-window functional commits) and continuedev (no in-window
> commits) contribute nothing this cycle.

## Executive Summary

- **2/5 carry-overs from 2026-06-02 closed (Items #2 and #5).** Three
  remain open:
  - **Item #1** (shell-startup + build-tool config protection) - still
    only `.kilo/`/`.alexi/`/`kilo.json`/`AGENTS.md` are protected
    (`src/permission/config-paths.ts:20-28`); `.zshenv`, `.npmrc`, etc.
    are unguarded. **Re-promoted as Item #1 below** with extra urgency
    because v2.1.166 added more security teeth (see next bullet).
  - **Item #3** (typed `TimeoutError`). `src/utils/errors.ts` does not
    exist; `browser.ts:232,276` still uses string `err.name === 'TimeoutError'`
    checks. **Re-listed as Item #3 below.**
  - **Item #4** (`examples/plugins/env-blocker/`). No `examples/` directory
    in repo. **Re-listed as Item #4 below.**
- **claude-code v2.1.166 - `fallbackModel` chain (up to 3) (medium)**: the
  marquee feature in this window. Anthropics shipped a `fallbackModel`
  setting that accepts a list of up to three models tried in order when
  the primary is overloaded or unavailable; `--fallback-model` now also
  applies to interactive sessions. Our state today is a SINGLE-string
  `fallbackModel` in `src/config/routingConfig.ts:29` (`fallbackModel:
  string`), used only when the *primary id is not recognized*
  (`src/providers/index.ts:90-96`) - **not** when the primary is reachable
  but returns a 5xx/overloaded response. Item #2 below.
- **claude-code v2.1.166 - glob pattern support in deny rule tool-name
  position (small)**: `"*"` (or any glob) as the tool-name in a deny rule
  now denies all tools; allow rules reject non-MCP globs; unknown tool
  names in deny rules warn at startup. Our `PermissionRule.tools` already
  accepts patterns and we use `matchPattern` from `src/permission/wildcard.ts`
  (`src/permission/index.ts:383-384`), so the **matching engine is already
  there** - what is missing is (a) startup validation that warns on unknown
  tool names in deny rules, (b) the asymmetric guard that *rejects* allow
  rules with non-MCP globs (you cannot say "allow *", because that defeats
  the deny side), and (c) an explicit test that `tools: ["*"]` denies every
  tool. Item #5 below (small, polish).
- **claude-code v2.1.166 - hardened cross-session messaging (informational)**:
  messages relayed via `SendMessage` from other Claude sessions no longer
  carry user authority - receivers refuse relayed permission requests, and
  auto mode blocks them. We don't ship cross-session messaging
  (`grep cross.*session src/core/sessionManager.ts` returns nothing). N/A.
- **claude-code v2.1.166 - `MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0` and `--thinking disabled`
  per-model toggle (informational)**: disables thinking on think-by-default
  models via the Claude API. Our reasoning controls (`reasoningEffort`,
  `effortLevel.ts`) are level-based, not a hard 0/disabled toggle.
  Tracker.
- **claude-code v2.1.166 - retry-on-fallback for non-retryable errors
  (informational)**: claude-code retries a turn once on the *fallback model*
  when the API rejects an unexpected non-retryable error; auth, rate-limit,
  request-size, transport surface immediately. Coupled with Item #2 below
  (multi-model fallback). Same blast-radius analysis applies.
- **claude-code v2.1.163 - `Stop`/`SubagentStop` `additionalContext` (small)**:
  Stop and SubagentStop hooks can now return
  `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` to give Claude feedback and **keep
  the turn going** without being labeled a hook error. Today our
  `HookSpecificOutputSchema` (`src/hooks/index.ts:111-114`) only declares
  `reloadSkills` and `sessionTitle`. The SessionStart-hook reload-skills
  plumbing is the obvious template. Item #6 below.
- **claude-code v2.1.163 - `/plugin list --enabled/--disabled` (informational)**:
  identical idea to our `/plugins` REPL command (shipped this cycle). Add
  `--enabled` / `--disabled` flags to `alexi plugin list` and pass-through
  through the slash command. Tracker, low-priority polish.
- **claude-code v2.1.163 - stdio MCP servers receive
  `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` on `--resume` (verified ours)**: same as cline
  semantics. We inject `ALEXI_SESSION_ID` at `src/mcp/client.ts:47,177`.
  What we don't do is verify the variable is *re-emitted* on `alexi sessions
  resume <id>`. Need a smoke test or a unit test confirming the resume code
  path passes the resumed session id into `McpConnectOptions.sessionId`.
  Tracker.
- **claude-code v2.1.163 - hook `if: "Bash(...)"` matches inside
  subshells/backticks (small)**: 2.1.163 fixed a regression where the
  Bash-condition pattern fired on every command that contained `$()` or
  `$VAR`; the pattern now matches commands inside subshells/backticks
  consistently. We do not have a hook-condition expression language at all
  (`grep -rn "Bash(" src/hooks/` returns nothing) - hook gating is by tool
  name only (`HookDefinitionSchema.tools`). Tracker for a future
  hook-conditions feature; not actionable this cycle.
- **claude-code v2.1.162 - `claude agents --json waitingFor` (tracker)**:
  shows what a waiting session is blocked on (e.g. permission prompt). Our
  equivalent surface would be `alexi sessions list --json` adding a
  `waitingFor` field on sessions paused on a permission ask. Marginal
  utility without an `agents`-style top-level dispatch UI. Tracker.
- **claude-code v2.1.162 - WebFetch deny/ask/allow rules take precedence
  over preapproved-host auto-allow (small)**: today our preapproved-host
  list (if any) is silent - `grep preapproved src/permission/ src/tool/tools/webfetch.ts`
  returns nothing, so we don't ship preapproved-host auto-allow at all.
  Defer until/unless we add it.
- **cline #11161 - skills bundled with plugins (medium)**: cline shipped
  *file-backed* skill bundling - a plugin package can include a `skills/`
  directory (e.g. `triage/SKILL.md`) and the loader picks it up
  automatically by walking package roots, with no explicit registration
  call. Our plugin manifest already accepts an inline `skills:` array
  (`src/plugin/index.ts:1080-1085`), but inline declarations don't scale to
  multi-skill plugins (frontmatter, progressive-disclosure files, package
  assets). Wiring `<plugin-root>/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` into the existing
  skill loader's search roots is the analogous primitive. Item #7 below.
- **cline #11247 - `cline plugin uninstall` + aliases (small)**: first-class
  uninstall path with `--json` and `--cwd <path>`, plus a settings TUI row
  showing `D delete` with a confirmation dialog. Our `alexi plugin` command
  has `init` (scaffolder) only - `grep uninstall src/cli/commands/plugin.ts`
  returns nothing. Users today must `rm -rf .alexi/skills/<name>/` manually.
  Item #8 below.
- **cline #11230 - install plugins by official slug (small/medium)**: `cline
  plugin install web-search` resolves to `plugins/web-search` in
  `github.com/cline/plugins.git`, with explicit forms (`npm:`, `--git`,
  paths) preserved. We don't ship `alexi plugin install` at all yet, so
  this is a *natural-but-larger* dependency on first standing up a curated
  registry. Tracker / future-RFC, not a research action.
- **cline #11225 - union schema for read-files tool input (informational)**:
  cline accepts both `{ filepath: string }` and `{ filepaths: string[] }`
  via a discriminated union after multi-file read regressions. Our `read.ts`
  takes a single `filePath`. Adding a `filePaths: string[]` variant means
  one Zod `union` and a parallel-read loop. Marginal until a model emits
  the multi-file shape. Tracker.
- **cline #11244 - `skills` plugin capability (informational)**: plugin
  manifest validation accepts `capabilities: ["skills"]`. We don't have a
  `capabilities` validation list - our `PluginManifestSchema` is open to
  any field. Pairs naturally with cline #11161 (Item #7) and is part of
  the same ~half-day of work.
- **cline #11240 - guard MCP settings writes against watcher races (small)**:
  cline #11240 found a race where the file-watcher could empty the MCP
  list between a `delete` and the post-delete `add` write; the fix is a
  single in-flight write lock. Our MCP config persistence is at
  `src/mcp/config.ts`. Need to verify whether we have a similar
  read-modify-write window. Tracker (audit).
- **cline #11258 - typed `AgentRuntimeAbortError` for runtime aborts (informational)**:
  cline introduced a typed abort error so the daemon's
  `unhandledRejection` boundary can ignore *only* abort rejections, not
  all errors. Mirrors our 2026-06-02 Item #3 (`TimeoutError`) pattern. If
  we ship #3 below, opening a follow-up `AbortError` typed error is the
  same one-file change. Note for the followup PR.
- **cline #11259 - recover stale interactive sessions; suppress shutdown
  hook races (small)**: when a session id no longer exists in the
  underlying daemon (after a hub restart), cline's CLI now detects "session
  not found", recovers by starting a fresh session, and silences late hook
  dispatches that race shutdown. Our `src/core/sessionManager.ts` has no
  notion of a long-lived daemon, so the precise issue does not apply -
  but the pattern (recover gracefully when a referenced session id has
  vanished from disk) does. We surface a hard error if `loadSession(id)`
  finds nothing. Item #9 below.
- **cline #11265 - clear abort indicator immediately on Esc (informational)**:
  TUI-only - hide the "Thinking... (esc to cancel)" indicator the moment
  abort is reported, not after the backend's terminal events finish
  unwinding. Our TUI's spinner hide path is in
  `src/cli/tui/components/Spinner.tsx`/`StatusLine.tsx`. Worth a TUI
  smoke-check on Esc-during-stream behaviour. Tracker.
- **cline #11220 - skills in slash autocomplete (informational)**: cline's
  `/skills` picker silently disabled the runtime skill commands' `visible`
  flag, hiding them from `/`-autocomplete. Our `/skills` REPL command was
  just shipped this cycle (`src/cli/interactive.ts:2161`) - we don't have
  a skills picker with a `visible` flag. N/A.
- **cline #11273 - normalize Slack channel mentions to original post
  thread (informational)**: connector-specific. We don't ship a Slack
  connector. N/A.
- **cline #11256 - Telegram `--allowed-user-id` flag (informational)**:
  connector-specific. N/A. Pattern (first-class flag for a common
  hook-command shape) is worth remembering if we ever ship connectors.
- **cline #11227 - `baseUrl` on Anthropic-vendor providers (small)**: cline
  was ignoring `baseUrl` on Anthropic-protocol providers, sending MiniMax
  keys to `api.anthropic.com`. Our `src/providers/index.ts` provider
  registry: check whether SAP-AI-Core deployments accept `baseUrl`
  override. We use `getProviderForModel` to dispatch, and SAP deployments
  encode the URL via `AICORE_DEPLOYMENT_ID`. The exact bug doesn't apply
  to us, but a *negative test* (resolve provider for `mistral-large` via
  proxy and confirm `baseUrl` resolves through `SAP_PROXY_*`) is worth
  adding. Tracker.
- **cline #11235 - route custom registered handlers through agent
  runtime (informational)**: cline closed a gap where handlers registered
  via `registerHandler(providerId, factory)` were honoured by `createHandler`
  callers but not the agent runtime. Pattern: lazy adapter inside `stream`.
  We don't have a public `registerHandler` SPI today; provider registration
  is via `getProviderForModel` dispatch. If we add a plugin-contributed
  provider surface (cline shipped exactly this in #11234, see below), this
  is the chokepoint we'd refactor. Tracker.
- **cline #11234 - custom model provider plugin example (informational)**:
  cline added an `openrouter-provider.ts` SDK example demonstrating the
  `providers` plugin capability via `Llms.registerProvider(...)`. Our
  plugin system supports `tools`, `skills`, `commands`, hooks, and rules,
  but not `providers`. Item #10 below as a cycle-closing tracker - we
  should at least scope the `providers` capability shape because it's the
  obvious next step after cline #11161 (skills) + #11244 (capabilities
  list).
- **kilocode v7.3.33-v7.3.40 (Jun 5-6)**: a release flurry around
  Bun-baseline-startup crashes (#10957, #10958), morphsdk CJS resolution
  (#10956), CLI release validation (#10955), VS Code sidebar reload
  preservation (#10951), MCP JSON strict output (#10933), webfetch
  unsupported images (#10901), and chat layout reservations (#10950).
  None of these are portable to Alexi (Bun/morphsdk/VS-Code product
  specific). The notable one is **#10952 (custom plan exit paths)**:
  `cline plan` flow allows exiting from a planning mode at custom paths.
  We don't have a planning *mode* (we have stages via `stageManager.ts`),
  so the exit-path config doesn't translate. N/A.
- **aider (no in-window functional commits)** and **continuedev (no
  in-window commits)** - nothing to act on.

## Detailed Findings

### Closed since 2026-06-02 (verified in code)

- **Item #2: `ALEXI_PLUGIN_RULE_TIMEOUT_MS` env override** -
  `src/plugin/__tests__/ruleCommandRunner.test.ts:269` (full describe block
  covering env-only timeout, per-call override winning, invalid env values
  ignored, and zero/negative values ignored). The implementation lives in
  `src/plugin/ruleCommandRunner.ts` (env read at module load + a precedence
  branch in `runRuleCommand`).
- **Item #5: `/plugins` and `/skills` REPL slash commands** -
  `src/cli/interactive.ts:240-245` (help-text entries),
  `:316` (exported for unit tests), `:2115-2156` (`/plugins` cases with
  `toggle <name>` subcommand), `:2161-2196` (`/skills` analogous), all
  reusing the existing `listPlugins` / `listSkills` CLI command code.

### Still open from 2026-06-02

- **Item #1**: shell-startup + build-tool config protection. Re-listed as
  Item #1 below.
- **Item #3**: typed `TimeoutError`. Re-listed as Item #3 below.
- **Item #4**: `examples/plugins/env-blocker/` reference plugin. Re-listed
  as Item #4 below.

### New gaps (sorted by leverage)

#### 1. Shell-startup + build-tool config protection - RE-PROMOTED (Category: permission - small/medium, ~6h)

- **What**: Same as 2026-06-02 Item #1. Extend
  `src/permission/config-paths.ts` to protect shell-startup files
  (`~/.zshenv`, `~/.zlogin`, `~/.bash_login`, `~/.bash_profile`,
  `~/.config/fish/config.fish`, etc.), git config (`~/.gitconfig`,
  `~/.config/git/`), and build-tool exec-paths (`.npmrc`, `.yarnrc*`,
  `bunfig.toml`, `.bazelrc`, `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `.devcontainer/`).
- **Why**: Two cycles since this gap was identified; now reinforced by
  v2.1.166 adding `MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0` semantics (which makes more
  agent classes able to reason about which paths to write) and by
  v2.1.166 hardening cross-session messaging (which is the same blast-radius
  story: an exfil-payload in `~/.zshenv` survives the agent process).
  This is the only finding open against us that closes a real security
  gap rather than a quality-of-life one.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code CHANGELOG v2.1.160; carried forward
  through v2.1.161-v2.1.167 unchanged.
- **Size**: small/medium (~6h). See 2026-06-02 Item #1 implementation
  sketch - it stands as written.

#### 2. Multi-model `fallbackModel` chain (Category: provider/config - medium, ~1d)

- **What**: Extend `RoutingPreferences.fallbackModel` to accept either a
  string OR a `string[]` of up to N (claude-code uses 3) candidate fallback
  models tried in order on overload/unavailable. Today
  (`src/config/routingConfig.ts:29`):
  ```ts
  fallbackModel: string;
  ```
  After:
  ```ts
  fallbackModel: string | string[];
  ```
  Plus a runtime path: when streaming/orchestration receives a 5xx/429/529
  or "overloaded" body from the primary model, switch to fallback[0]; if
  that *also* fails, fallback[1]; and so on. Today the fallback resolver
  (`src/providers/index.ts:90-96`) only fires when the **primary id is
  unrecognized** (no provider for it), not on transient errors.
- **Why**: SAP-AI-Core deployments occasionally 503 (per earlier research
  cycles tracking SAP outages), and our orchestration today returns the
  raw error to the user instead of trying a configured peer. The
  multi-model variant is materially more useful than a single fallback
  because the most likely fallback (Sonnet 4.6) shares the same SAP
  outage window as Opus 4.7; a third-tier (e.g. `gpt-4o` via OpenAI proxy
  or `mistral-large` via SAP-AI-Core) is what actually recovers a session.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.166 CHANGELOG: "Added
  `fallbackModel` setting to configure up to three fallback models tried
  in order when the primary model is overloaded or unavailable;
  `--fallback-model` now also applies to interactive sessions".
- **Size**: medium (~1 day). The schema change is one line. The runtime
  chain logic is the larger half: `streamingOrchestrator.ts` needs an
  outer retry-with-next-model loop wrapping the streaming call, with the
  same overload-detection logic (`error.statusCode in [429, 502, 503,
  504, 529]` plus body markers like "overloaded_error"). Reuse
  `src/core/error-backoff.ts` if it has retry-budget plumbing.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - `RoutingPreferencesSchema` accepts `z.union([z.string(),
    z.array(z.string()).max(3)])`.
  - `resolveFallbackModelId` becomes `resolveFallbackModelChain` returning
    `string[]`; the single-string case wraps in a 1-element array.
  - In `streamingOrchestrator.ts`, on a stream-level overload, walk the
    chain (with the original `fallbackModel` semantics still applying as
    the "id-not-recognized" path on each candidate).
  - `--fallback-model <list>` CLI flag accepts a comma-separated list.
  - Telemetry: emit a `provider.fallback` event with `from`, `to`, `reason`,
    `attempt` so we can see how often this fires in the wild.

#### 3. Typed `TimeoutError` - RE-PROMOTED (Category: tool - small, ~3h)

- **What**: Same as 2026-06-02 Item #3. Add `class TimeoutError extends
  Error` with `kind: 'TimeoutError'` brand in `src/utils/errors.ts`.
  Refactor `bash.ts`, `webfetch.ts`, `shell.ts`, `diagnostics.ts`,
  `browser.ts:232,276` (`err.name === 'TimeoutError'` -> `err instanceof
  TimeoutError`).
- **Why**: Doubles down with cline #11258's `AgentRuntimeAbortError`
  pattern - the same "typed control-flow error so unhandled-rejection
  boundaries can distinguish abort/timeout from real failure" shape.
  Today `bash.ts` returns `success: false, error: 'Command exited with
  code -1'` for both SIGTERM-from-timeout and a genuine `process.exit(-1)`,
  which the agent cannot distinguish; this is now a TWO-precedent gap
  (claude/cline both ship typed errors here).
- **Source**: cline/cline#11149 (typed `TimeoutError`) + cline/cline#11258
  (typed `AgentRuntimeAbortError`).
- **Size**: small (~3h). See 2026-06-02 Item #3 sketch.
- **Adjustment vs prior cycle**: open the follow-up `AbortError` typed
  error in the same PR series (file as Item #3.1 in tasks), since the
  cline #11258 pattern shows the two errors travel together.

#### 4. `examples/plugins/env-blocker/` reference plugin - RE-PROMOTED (Category: plugin - small, ~4h)

- **What**: Same as 2026-06-02 Item #4. Add a working
  `examples/plugins/env-blocker/` demonstrating a `PreToolUse` hook that
  returns `decision: 'block'` for any tool call touching secret-bearing
  `.env*` files. Cover both file tools and shell tools (via
  `src/permission/shell-parser.ts`).
- **Why**: Two cycles since flagged, and now cline ships an entire
  *family* of plugin examples (#11234 `openrouter-provider.ts`, #11192
  `env-blocker`). We're now visibly behind on plugin-author-facing
  documentation: the plugin scaffolder ships, the rule contributions
  surface ships, but there is no end-to-end worked example a contributor
  can study.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11192 (env-blocker) + cline/cline#11234
  (openrouter-provider).
- **Size**: small (~4h). See 2026-06-02 Item #4 sketch.
- **Adjustment vs prior cycle**: produce TWO examples in the same PR -
  `env-blocker/` (security policy enforcement) AND `tool-logger/` (a
  no-op `PostToolUse` hook that writes a JSONL audit log). The two
  together exercise both `decision: block` and the success path through
  the hook plumbing.

#### 5. Glob-aware deny-rule warnings + asymmetric allow-rule guard (Category: permission - small, ~2h)

- **What**: Three small changes around `PermissionRule.tools`:
  1. **Startup warning**: when loading rules, for every deny rule with a
     `tools` entry that does NOT match any registered tool name and does
     NOT contain a glob (`*`/`?`), emit a one-line warning at startup
     (`logger.warn`). Today an entry like `tools: ['Bsah']` is silently
     a no-op.
  2. **Asymmetric guard**: in *allow* rules with a `tools` entry, reject
     glob patterns (`*`/`?`) at schema-validation time UNLESS the tool
     name is prefixed `mcp__` (cline/claude convention for MCP-namespaced
     tools). This prevents `tools: ['*'], decision: 'allow'` from
     accidentally turning off the deny-secrets rule.
  3. **Test**: add a test asserting `tools: ['*'], decision: 'deny'`
     denies every tool call (via `evaluateRule`), confirming the matching
     engine treats `*` correctly.
- **Why**: claude-code v2.1.166 shipped exactly these three guards in the
  same release; the "wildcard `*` denies all" capability is implicit in
  our `matchPattern` behaviour but is not part of our test surface, and
  the typo-warning + asymmetric-glob guard close real footguns that we
  inherited the moment we accepted `tools: string[]` of patterns.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.166 CHANGELOG:
  "Added glob pattern support in deny rule tool-name position (`*`
  denies all tools); allow rules reject non-MCP globs, and unknown tool
  names in deny rules warn at startup".
- **Size**: small (~2h). The matching is already there; this is purely
  validation + warning + tests.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - Extend `PermissionRuleSchema.refine(...)` (or a wrapper) to call a
    `validateAllowRuleGlobs(rule, registry)` that throws a Zod error on
    glob-in-allow unless `mcp__`-prefixed.
  - In `PermissionManager.constructor`, after `sortRules`, walk all
    rules; for each `decision: 'deny'` rule with literal tool names,
    cross-check against `getAllToolNames()` (already imported in
    `src/tool/registry.ts`); warn for unknowns.
  - Tests under `src/permission/__tests__/wildcard-tools.test.ts`.

#### 6. `Stop`/`SubagentStop` `additionalContext` (Category: hooks - small, ~3h)

- **What**: Extend `HookSpecificOutputSchema` (`src/hooks/index.ts:111-114`)
  to include `additionalContext: z.string().optional()`. When emitted by
  a `Stop` hook (or a future `SubagentStop` event - we don't have one
  yet, but the field is harmless on others), the orchestrator appends
  the string to the next-turn context as a system message and **does
  not** count the hook as a block (no STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP increment, no
  `HookFailed` event).
- **Why**: This unlocks the most useful Stop-hook pattern: a quick lint
  / tests / type-check pass that tells the model *what to fix* without
  the harsher "hook failed, retry" semantics. Today our Stop hook
  returning non-zero counts toward the block cap; there's no way for a
  hook to say "I noticed something but the turn is fine to end - here's
  feedback for the model."
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.163 CHANGELOG: "Stop and
  SubagentStop hooks can now return `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext`
  to give Claude feedback and keep the turn going without being labeled
  a hook error".
- **Size**: small (~3h). One schema field + one branch in
  `runHook`/`runStopHooks` (whichever module aggregates Stop results)
  that, when `additionalContext` is set, marks the result as success and
  injects the string. Tests at `src/hooks/__tests__/additional-context.test.ts`.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - `HookSpecificOutputSchema = z.object({ reloadSkills:
    z.boolean().optional(), sessionTitle: z.string().optional(),
    additionalContext: z.string().optional() })`.
  - In `runStopHooks` (search for `STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP` consumer),
    treat `result.hookSpecificOutput?.additionalContext` like a
    successful result that contributes context.
  - In `agenticChat.ts` / `streamingOrchestrator.ts`, when assembling the
    next turn's context, append all collected `additionalContext` strings
    as a system note ("Hook feedback (non-blocking): ...").
  - Test pattern mirrors `reload-skills.test.ts` exactly.

#### 7. File-backed plugin-bundled skills (`<plugin>/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`) (Category: plugin - medium, ~6h)

- **What**: Today plugins can declare inline skills (`plugin.skills:
  [SkillDef, ...]` at `src/plugin/index.ts:1080-1085`), but cannot bundle
  a *directory* of skills the way file-backed skills work in the rest of
  the system. Wire the plugin loader so that for each loaded plugin
  manifest at `<root>/plugin.json`, we add `<root>/skills/` to the
  skill-loader's search roots, with the same SKILL.md frontmatter +
  instruction-body parsing the existing skill loader uses.
- **Why**: cline #11161 made this the canonical pattern: "Plugin authors
  do not need to call a registration API, copy files into a managed
  config directory, or invent a plugin-specific skill format." Multi-skill
  plugins (e.g. a `linear-tools` plugin with `triage`, `breakdown`,
  `escalate` skills) are the obvious shape; inline declarations break
  down on progressive-disclosure files (`SKILL.md` + `notes.md` +
  `templates/`) and on package assets. The mechanism we already have
  (skill-loader search roots) needs only one new contributor.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11161 ("feat: add skills bundled with plugins").
- **Size**: medium (~6h). The package-root derivation cline mentions in
  the PR description doesn't apply to us because `.alexi/skills/<name>/
  plugin.json` already IS the package root - there's no install-staging
  layer to walk through. So our version is strictly simpler: register
  `<plugin-root>/skills/` as a search root if the directory exists.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - In the plugin loader (the `loadPlugin` chain in `src/plugin/index.ts`),
    when a plugin has been resolved, push `path.join(state.root,
    'skills')` to the skill manager's search-root list IF that directory
    exists.
  - The skill manager re-scans on `reloadSkills`, so newly-installed
    plugins surface their bundled skills automatically.
  - Tests: `src/plugin/__tests__/bundled-skills.test.ts` creating a tmp
    `.alexi/skills/foo/plugin.json` plus `.alexi/skills/foo/skills/
    bar/SKILL.md`, loading the plugin manager, and asserting `bar` is
    in the loaded-skills list.
  - Pair with cline #11244: add a `capabilities: z.array(z.enum(['tools',
    'skills', 'commands', 'rules', 'hooks', 'providers']))` field on
    `PluginManifestSchema` that is *informational only* (advertises what
    a plugin contributes; does not gate runtime behaviour). Closes the
    #11244 footgun preemptively.

#### 8. `alexi plugin uninstall` (Category: command - small, ~3h)

- **What**: Add `alexi plugin uninstall <name>` (aliases: `remove`, `rm`)
  to `src/cli/commands/plugin.ts`. Removes the plugin's
  `.alexi/skills/<name>/` (project) or `~/.alexi/skills/<name>/` (global)
  directory after confirming non-empty plugin entries inside, with a
  `--global` flag mirroring `init`. `--json` output for scripts. After
  delete, fire the existing reload-skills/instructions paths so removed
  plugin tools or bundled skills stop being available without a CLI
  restart. Add the corresponding `/plugins uninstall <name>` REPL form.
- **Why**: Symmetry with `alexi plugin init` (shipped two cycles ago).
  Today removing a plugin requires `rm -rf .alexi/skills/<name>/` plus
  hand-editing settings if the plugin appears in disabled lists. cline
  #11247 documents the exact pain point and shipped the corresponding
  fix.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11247.
- **Size**: small (~3h). The function body is ~30 lines (resolve path,
  refuse to delete the plugin's parent if it shares with siblings,
  `fs.rm({ recursive: true, force: false })`, log + return JSON).
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - New exported `uninstallPlugin(name, opts, cwd, homeDir)` in
    `plugin.ts` with the same `(name, opts, cwd, homeDir)` shape as
    `scaffoldPlugin`.
  - Resolve via the same global/project preference logic.
  - Tests: `src/cli/commands/__tests__/plugin-uninstall.test.ts`
    covering happy path, refuse-on-missing, refuse-on-non-plugin-dir,
    `--global` switch, idempotency (uninstalling an already-removed
    plugin returns a clean error code).

#### 9. Stale-session recovery on `alexi sessions resume` (Category: command - small, ~3h)

- **What**: When `alexi sessions resume <id>` (or `chat --session <id>`)
  is called with an id that no longer exists on disk, today we throw.
  Adopt cline #11259's pattern: detect "session not found", log a
  warning, and start a fresh session that records *why* the prior id
  wasn't usable (so users know whether to retry or to assume their
  history is gone).
- **Why**: External tools (autohealer, agent factory, IDE extensions)
  hold onto session ids across `~/.alexi/sessions/` rotations or backups.
  When the id evaporates - a manual cleanup, a disk full event - the
  current behaviour is a hard error; the fix is graceful degradation
  with a clear log.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11259 ("fix(cli): recover stale interactive
  sessions and suppress shutdown races").
- **Size**: small (~3h). One branch in `loadSession()` (or wherever
  resume resolves the id) to fall through to `createSession(...)` with
  a `recoveredFrom: id` field, plus a CLI message.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - In `src/core/sessionManager.ts:loadSession`, replace the "throw on
    missing" path with `return { ...newSession, recoveredFrom: id }`
    when the caller passed `{ allowRecovery: true }`.
  - In `src/cli/commands/chat.ts` and `src/cli/commands/sessions.ts`
    resume handler, pass `allowRecovery: true` and surface a warning
    line ("Session <id> not found - starting fresh; previous history
    is unrecoverable").
  - Tests: `src/cli/commands/__tests__/sessions-resume.recover.test.ts`
    creating a `tmpdir` `~/.alexi/sessions/`, attempting resume with a
    non-existent id, and asserting (a) no throw, (b) new session has
    `recoveredFrom` set, (c) listSessions shows both (the original
    never existed, the new one shows up with the marker).

#### 10. `providers` plugin capability - TRACKER (Category: plugin - medium/large, ~2d)

- **What**: Add a `providers` capability to the plugin manifest so a
  plugin can register a new model provider (the way cline #11234's
  `openrouter-provider.ts` example does), with a single chokepoint
  (`createAgentModelFromConfig`-style adapter) that builds a provider
  instance on demand. This is the *natural next step* after Items #6
  and #7 and after our `tools`/`skills`/`commands`/`hooks`/`rules`
  capabilities.
- **Why**: Plugin-contributed providers let third-party deployments be
  added without forking Alexi, and they're the right shape for "use my
  internal SAP-AI-Core deployment with custom auth" use cases. cline
  shipped both the capability registration (#11244) and a working example
  (#11234) in this window.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11234 + cline/cline#11235.
- **Size**: medium-large (~2 days), out of envelope for this cycle.
  Tracker: file as a multi-PR initiative under
  `auto-implement` after the `capabilities:` field lands (Item #7's
  sub-item).

### Notable upstream items deliberately deferred

- **claude-code v2.1.167 - bug fixes / reliability** (no detail in
  CHANGELOG). Re-evaluate next cycle.
- **claude-code v2.1.165 - bug fixes / reliability** (no detail). N/A.
- **claude-code v2.1.166 - hardened cross-session messaging** - we don't
  ship cross-session messaging.
- **claude-code v2.1.166 - JetBrains terminal flicker fix** - IDE-specific.
- **claude-code v2.1.166 - Kitty keyboard protocol Shift+non-ASCII** -
  terminal-specific.
- **claude-code v2.1.166 - PowerShell command validation hang** - Windows
  PowerShell-specific; we use Node `child_process` directly.
- **claude-code v2.1.166 - macOS bg-pty-host CPU spin** - macOS-specific
  background daemon.
- **claude-code v2.1.166 - voice mode `/login` clear** - we don't ship
  voice mode.
- **claude-code v2.1.163 - `claude -p` background-shell timeout** - we
  don't ship `-p` print mode with backgrounded shells.
- **claude-code v2.1.163 - bazel/EDR `$TMPDIR` regression** - environment
  -specific; we don't override `$TMPDIR`.
- **claude-code v2.1.163 - Windows OneDrive read-only `EEXIST`** -
  Windows-OneDrive-specific.
- **claude-code v2.1.163 - org-managed permission rules during config-fetch
  race** - we don't have managed-settings fetching.
- **claude-code v2.1.162 - WebFetch preapproved-host precedence** - we
  don't ship preapproved hosts.
- **claude-code v2.1.162 - Windows backslash permission rule matching** -
  worth one defensive `path.posix.normalize` audit; tracker.
- **claude-code v2.1.162 - LSP `workspaceSymbol` `query` parameter** - we
  don't ship LSP tools.
- **claude-code v2.1.162 - background-service binary install delay** -
  we don't ship a background service.
- **cline #11211 (v3.87.0 release prep)** - non-functional.
- **cline #11233 (greptile/kanban repo housekeeping)** - non-functional.
- **cline #11252 (`bun run fix`)** - cosmetic.
- **cline #11236 (VSCode CI fix)** - VSCode-CI-specific.
- **cline #11173 (Fireworks model registry sync)** - product-specific.
- **cline #11242 (cline provider migration)** - cline-product-specific.
- **cline #11200 (move `sdk/apps/` to `apps/`)** - SDK reorg, not a feature.
- **cline #11161 SKILL.md frontmatter parsing details** - covered by Item #7;
  no separate finding.
- **cline #11220 skills in slash autocomplete** - Cline regression fix
  for a feature we never shipped.
- **cline #11211 / #11315 / #11316 / #11318 (release prep + recommended-models
  flag removal + version bump + debug section)** - non-functional.
- **cline #11280 (publish-cli skill ordering)** - cline release-process doc.
- **cline #11284 / #11285 (Windows test skip + npm update for auto updates)**
  - test infrastructure / cline auto-update specific.
- **kilocode v7.3.33-v7.3.40 fixes (Bun splitter, morphsdk CJS, MCP JSON
  strict, sidebar reload, webfetch images, custom plan exit paths)** -
  kilocode-product-specific. None portable to Alexi.
- **POSIX `cd`/`pushd`/subshell permission audit** - now THREE cycles
  open without an issue; carry forward and surface in the planning doc.
- **claude-code security-guidance plugin port to Alexi** - tracker; ties
  into Items #4 and #6.

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Land the small-wins cycle (~1.5 days total)**:
   - **Item #5** (deny-rule glob warnings + allow-rule asymmetric guard).
     ~2h, validation + tests only, no runtime behaviour change.
   - **Item #6** (`Stop` `additionalContext`). ~3h, follows the
     existing `reload-skills` template.
   - **Item #8** (`alexi plugin uninstall`). ~3h, symmetric with `init`.
   - **Item #9** (stale-session recovery). ~3h, single-branch change.
   These four are independent (different directories, no coupling); each
   ships as its own PR.

2. **Item #2 (multi-model `fallbackModel` chain) is the cycle's marquee
   feature**. Plan ~1 day. The schema change is one line; the runtime
   chain logic is the larger half. SAP-AI-Core 503 windows make this a
   user-visible reliability improvement.

3. **Items #1 (shell-startup protection) and #3 (typed `TimeoutError`)
   are now in their THIRD cycle open**. Both need to ship next cycle at
   the latest. Item #1 because every cycle without it expands the
   blast radius; Item #3 because cline #11258 made the precedent
   two-precedented (`TimeoutError` + `AgentRuntimeAbortError` travel
   together).

4. **Item #4 (`examples/plugins/`) is the cycle's documentation
   deliverable**. Pair `env-blocker/` with `tool-logger/` so contributors
   see both the block path and the audit path.

5. **Item #7 (file-backed plugin-bundled skills + `capabilities` array)
   is the natural follow-up to the prior cycle's plugin-rule
   contributions**. Plan ~6h.

6. **Item #10 (`providers` plugin capability) is a tracker**, not this
   cycle's deliverable. File a multi-PR initiative under `auto-implement`
   after Item #7's `capabilities:` field lands.

7. **File the deferred audits** that have been carrying forward without
   tickets: POSIX `cd`/`pushd`/subshell permission audit (third cycle
   open), `background-process` port-scan opt-in (kilocode #10796),
   subagent-running spinner (kilocode #10310), MCP `--resume` session-id
   re-emission (claude-code v2.1.163).

8. **Tracker hygiene**: the 06-02 cycle had 5 new gaps; this cycle closed
   2 (40%) and re-promotes 3 (60%). Three carry-overs across a
   3-cycle window indicates either prioritisation drift on items #1/#3/#4
   or under-staffing of security-adjacent work. Surface this delta in
   the planning doc as a process signal.

9. **Re-evaluate next cycle**: SAP-AI-Core deployment outage frequency
   (validates Item #2), claude-code v2.1.165/v2.1.167 detail (currently
   "bug fixes" only), cline `providers` capability example uptake,
   plugin marketplace timeline.
